evidence: Statement bounds the least prime factor of the binomial coefficient C(n,k) by max(n/k,k).
verify_note: Decl Erdos1094.erdos_1094 exists (@[category research open]); QID live, label matches; binomial coefficient C(n,k) is the object of the statement.

evidence: The conjecture is about the least prime factor (minFac) of C(n,k) exceeding max(n/k,k) only finitely often.
verify_note: Same decl verified; least prime factor (minFac) is the quantity bounded in the statement; QID live, label matches.

evidence: g(k) is the least n>k+1 with all prime factors of the binomial coefficient C(n,k) exceeding k (Erdos-Selfridge function, OEIS A003458).
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified: file declares erdos_1095.variants.upper_conjecture inside namespace Erdos1095, so the FQN is Erdos1095.erdos_1095.variants.upper_conjecture; bare name does not exist.

evidence: Docstring: 'the smallest n such that all prime factors of C(n,k) are > k' (references ELS93 'least prime factor of a binomial coefficient').
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (missing enclosing namespace Erdos1095); FQN is Erdos1095.erdos_1095.variants.upper_conjecture.

evidence: Docstring: Erdos and Komornik proved the conclusion whenever 1<q<sqrt(q1), where q1 is the second Pisot-Vijayaraghavan number.
verify_note: Decl Erdos1096.erdos_1096 exists (@[category research solved]); QID live, label matches; second Pisot-Vijayaraghavan number is the threshold in the Erdos-Komornik resolution stated in the docstring — load-bearing, not scaffolding.

evidence: The conjecture counts distinct common differences of three-term arithmetic progressions in a finite integer set.
verify_note: Decl Erdos1097.erdos_1097 exists (@[category research solved]); statement counts common differences of three-term APs; QID live, label matches.

evidence: Docstring: 'Is every odd n > 1 the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2?'
verify_note: Decl Erdos11.erdos_11 exists (@[category research open]); Squarefree k is half the statement; QID live, label matches.

evidence: Docstring: 'Is every odd n > 1 the sum of a squarefree number and a power of 2?'
verify_note: Same decl verified; 2^l term is the other half of the statement; QID live, label matches.

evidence: The Granville-Soundararajan variant concludes infinitude of primes with 2^p ≡ 2 mod p^2 (the Wieferich condition; erdosproblems.com/11 lists OEIS A001220, the Wieferich primes).
verify_note: Decl Erdos11.erdos_11.variants.granville_soundararajan exists (FQN composes correctly; @[category research solved]); conclusion {p prime | 2^p = 2 mod p^2}.Infinite is exactly the Wieferich condition; QID live, label matches.

evidence: IsGood requires the sequence u to be pairwise coprime with convergent reciprocal sum, the core hypothesis of the problem.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified: declared inside namespace Erdos1101, FQN is Erdos1101.erdos_1101.parts.i; bare erdos_1101.parts.i does not exist.

evidence: Property P asks that {a in A | n + a is squarefree} be finite for every n ≥ 1.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1102); FQN is Erdos1102.erdos_1102.density_zero_of_P; bare name does not exist.

evidence: Docstring: 'If A has property P, then A has natural density 0.'
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1102); FQN is Erdos1102.erdos_1102.density_zero_of_P.

evidence: Docstring: 'Every sequence with property Q has upper density at most 6/pi^2.'
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1102); FQN is Erdos1102.erdos_1102.upper_density_Q.

evidence: Docstring: an infinite sequence inside SF, the set of squarefree numbers, with property Q and natural density 6/pi^2.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1102); FQN is Erdos1102.erdos_1102.lower_density_Q_exists.

evidence: f(n) is the maximum chromatic number of a triangle-free graph on n vertices (triangleFreeMaxChromatic).
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1104); FQN is Erdos1104.erdos_1104.variants.lower; bare name does not exist.

evidence: The extremal quantity ranges over graphs G with G.CliqueFree 3, i.e. triangle-free graphs.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1104); FQN is Erdos1104.erdos_1104.variants.lower.

evidence: Upper bound f(n) ≤ c2·sqrt(n/log n) on the maximum chromatic number of a triangle-free graph (Davies-Illingworth 2022).
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1104); FQN is Erdos1104.erdos_1104.variants.upper.

evidence: The bound is on triangleFreeMaxChromatic, the max chromatic number over G.CliqueFree 3 graphs.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1104); FQN is Erdos1104.erdos_1104.variants.upper.

evidence: Asks whether AR(n, C_k) = ((k-2)/2 + 1/(k-1))n + O(1) for the cycle C_k on k vertices.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1105); FQN is Erdos1105.erdos_1105.parts.i; bare name does not exist.

evidence: The anti-Ramsey number AR(n,G) is the max number of colours in an edge-colouring of K_n with no rainbow copy of G.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1105); FQN is Erdos1105.erdos_1105.parts.i.

evidence: Asks for the exact formula for AR(n, P_k) where P_k is the path on k vertices (announced by Yuan [Yu21]).
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1105); FQN is Erdos1105.erdos_1105.parts.ii.

evidence: AR(n, P_k) counts colours avoiding a rainbow path, per the anti-Ramsey definition in the docstring.
verify_note: Decl name not fully qualified (namespace Erdos1105); FQN is Erdos1105.erdos_1105.parts.ii.
